info.name: EscrowLedger Testing Collection
info.description:
Complete testing collection for Escrow-Ledger financial system

Covers:
- Auth Service seed and authentication
- Ledger seed data and balance verification
- Escrow creation (Buyer/Seller/Broker)
- Fee validation scenarios
- Happy flow (create → accept → complete → release)
- Dispute flow
- Internal transfers
- External transfers
- Edge cases

item.name: 0. Auth Service Seed

request.description:
Run Auth Service seed script to create test users

Users created:
- buyer_1, buyer_2 (buyer1@test.com, buyer2@test.com)
- seller_1, seller_2 (seller1@test.com, seller2@test.com)
- broker_1 (broker1@test.com)
- admin_1 (admin@test.com)

Password for all: Test123!@#
Admin password: Admin123!@#
Login as buyer_1 and store JWT token
Login as buyer_2 and store JWT token
Login as seller_1 and store JWT token
Login as seller_2 and store JWT token
Login as broker_1 and store JWT token
Login as admin and store JWT token

item.description: Seed Auth Service users and authenticate to get JWT tokens

item.name: 1. Ledger Seed

request.description:
Run Ledger Service seed script to create test accounts

Creates:
- buyer_1: spendable=150, reserved=0
- buyer_2: spendable=90, reserved=0
- seller_1: spendable=50, reserved=0
- seller_2: spendable=200, reserved=0
- broker_1: spendable=100, reserved=0
- System accounts: platform_revenue, platform_custody_pool, escrow_holding_pool
Verify buyer_1 has correct initial balance (150 spendable)
Verify seller_1 has correct initial balance (50 spendable)

item.description: Seed Ledger Service accounts and verify balances

item.name: 2. Buyer Creates Escrow

request.description:
Buyer creates escrow and pays full platform fee

Validation:
- Buyer balance >= escrow_amount + platform_fee (100 + 2 = 102)
- Buyer has 150, so should succeed

Buyer creates escrow with fee split 50/50

Validation:
- Buyer balance >= escrow_amount + buyer_fee_portion (100 + 1 = 101)
- Seller fee portion validated on accept

Buyer creates escrow, seller pays full fee

Validation:
- Buyer balance >= escrow_amount (100)
- Seller fee validated on accept

exec: pm.test("Error message mentions insufficient balance", function () {

request.description:
Buyer 2 tries to create escrow with amount > balance

Buyer 2 has 90, trying to create escrow for 200 + 2 fee = 202
Should fail with 400 Bad Request

item.description: Test escrow creation by buyer with different fee payment scenarios

item.name: 3. Seller Creates Escrow

body.raw:
{
  "buyerId": "{{buyer1_id}}",
  "sellerId": "{{seller1_id}}",
  "amount": 100,
  "asset": "USDT",
  "chain": "evm",
  "description": "Test escrow - Seller creates, seller pays fee",
  "feePaidBy": "seller",
  "platformFeeTotal": 2
}

request.description:
Seller creates escrow and pays full fee

CRITICAL Validation:
- Buyer balance >= escrow_amount (100) - CHECKED FIRST
- Seller balance >= platform_fee (2)
- Buyer has 150, Seller has 50, so should succeed

body.raw:
{
  "buyerId": "{{buyer1_id}}",
  "sellerId": "{{seller1_id}}",
  "amount": 100,
  "asset": "USDT",
  "chain": "evm",
  "description": "Test escrow - Seller creates, buyer pays fee",
  "feePaidBy": "buyer",
  "platformFeeTotal": 2
}

request.description:
Seller creates escrow, buyer pays full fee

CRITICAL Validation:
- Buyer balance >= escrow_amount + platform_fee (100 + 2 = 102)
- Buyer has 150, so should succeed

Seller creates escrow with fee split 50/50

CRITICAL Validation:
- Buyer balance >= escrow_amount + buyer_fee_portion (100 + 1 = 101)
- Seller balance >= seller_fee_portion (1)
- Buyer has 150, Seller has 50, so should succeed

body.raw:
{
  "buyerId": "{{buyer2_id}}",
  "sellerId": "{{seller1_id}}",
  "amount": 200,
  "asset": "USDT",
  "chain": "evm",
  "description": "Test escrow - Should fail (buyer lacks escrow amount)",
  "feePaidBy": "seller",
  "platformFeeTotal": 2
}

request.description:
Seller creates escrow, but buyer lacks escrow amount

CRITICAL: Buyer balance checked FIRST for escrow_amount
Buyer 2 has 90, escrow amount is 200
Should fail immediately with 400 Bad Request

Seller creates escrow but lacks fee amount

Seller has 50, fee is 100
Should fail with 400 Bad Request

item.description:
Test escrow creation by seller with different fee payment scenarios

CRITICAL: When seller creates escrow, buyer balance is ALWAYS checked first for escrow_amount

request.description:
Broker creates escrow with fee split

Validates both buyer and seller balances

item.name: 5. Happy Flow

request.description: Step 1: Create escrow

Step 2: Buyer records payment

This triggers Kafka event: escrow.payment.completed
Ledger service consumes and reserves funds

Step 2: Verify funds were reserved

After payment, Ledger should have:
- Buyer spendable: 150 - 100 = 50
- Buyer reserved: 0 + 100 = 100

Step 4: Seller accepts escrow

This triggers Kafka event: escrow.accepted

Step 5: Buyer records inspection (accepts)

This triggers Kafka event: escrow.inspection.completed
Ledger service consumes and releases funds to seller

exec: // Buyer reserved should be back to 0
// This test would need to check buyer balance separately

request.description:
Step 6: Verify funds released to seller

After inspection accepted, Ledger should have:
- Seller spendable: 50 + 100 = 150
- Platform revenue: 0 + 2 = 2

item.description: Complete happy flow: Create → Payment → Accept → Delivery → Inspection (Accept) → Release

item.name: 6. Dispute Flow

request.description: Create escrow for dispute testing
Record payment

Raise dispute

This triggers Kafka event: escrow.disputed
Ledger service consumes and freezes funds

Admin resolves dispute - refund buyer

This triggers Kafka event: escrow.resolved
Ledger service consumes and refunds funds to buyer

item.description: Dispute flow: Create → Payment → Dispute → Admin Resolve

item.name: 7. Internal Transfers (Service-to-Service)

request.description:
Internal transfer: Move funds from spendable to reserved

INTERNAL API: Requires service API key
NOT accessible to regular users
Internal transfer: Move funds from reserved to escrow holding pool
Internal transfer: Release funds from escrow holding to seller
Internal transfer: Refund funds from escrow holding to buyer
Internal transfer: Move platform fee to revenue account

Try to create duplicate transfer with same idempotency key

Should fail with 409 Conflict

Try to transfer more than available balance

Buyer 2 has 90, trying to transfer 1000
Should fail with 400 Bad Request

item.description:
Test internal transfers (service-to-service only)

These endpoints require service API key and are NOT accessible to regular users

request.description:
Create external transfer (payout to bank)

Flow:
1. Reserve funds internally
2. Create external_transfer record (PENDING)
3. Produce Kafka event: ExternalTransferRequested
4. Payment provider processes
5. Consume callback → Mark COMPLETED or FAILED

Simulate payment provider callback (success)

Marks transfer as COMPLETED

Simulate payment provider callback (failure)

Marks transfer as FAILED and releases reserved funds

Try to process duplicate callback

Should fail with 409 Conflict (idempotency)

item.name: 9. Edge Cases

request.description:
Try to create escrow with invalid fee split (percentages don't sum to 100)

Should fail with 400 Bad Request

Test concurrent escrow creation

Run multiple times simultaneously to test race conditions

exec: const totalBalance = response.balances.reduce((sum, b) => sum + b.balance, 0);

request.description:
Verify balances after operations

Check that:
- No negative balances
- Total balance = initial balance (no money creation/loss)
- Double-entry accounting maintained

item.description: Test edge cases and error scenarios
